text: With consistent training and racing over five decades, the age-related decline in marathon performance can be limited to a decrease in running speed of approximately 0.67% per year.
population: sub 3-hour marathoners with consistent multi-decade training

text: Sub 3-hour marathoners who maintained consistent training across five decades showed a total performance decline of approximately 26 minutes between their personal best and their worst performance in the fifth decade.
population: sub 3-hour marathoners with consistent multi-decade training

text: With consistent training, the age-related decline in marathon performance can be limited to less than 7% per decade at least until 60 years of age.
population: sub 3-hour marathoners with consistent multi-decade training

text: The average annual performance decline in consistently training marathoners corresponds to approximately 1 minute 4 seconds per year in marathon finish time.
population: sub 3-hour marathoners with consistent multi-decade training

text: Peak marathon performance in sub 3-hour runners occurs at approximately 28-29 years of age, with a mean personal best of 2:23 at age 28.6.

Longitudinal tracking of the same individuals provides more accurate estimates of age-related performance decline than cross-sectional analysis of age-group records, which belong to different individuals.
